nodes.name: NavArrow.swift
nodes.summary: Adaptive onboarding back/forward nav-arrow component family: a custom chevron Shape rendered as a spectrum-gradient stroked pill, with dark and light variants unified behind a colorScheme-aware OnboardingNavArrow wrapper.
nodes.languageNotes: Param ordering (size before action) is deliberate to enable trailing-closure call sites; body decomposed into computed sub-views to keep Swift type-checking fast.

nodes.name: NavArrowShape
nodes.summary: SwiftUI Shape that draws a double-line chevron glyph (ported from an HTML SVG viewBox) with all points proportional to the rect.

nodes.name: GradientStrokeArrow
nodes.summary: Renders NavArrowShape stroked with a configurable gradient plus two layered glow shadows.

nodes.name: DarkNavArrow
nodes.summary: Dark-mode nav arrow: a modal-background pill with a spectrum pillBorder, blurred glow duplicate, and centered spectrum arrow glyph.

nodes.name: LightNavArrow
nodes.summary: Light-mode nav arrow: card-background pill with aurora or magenta-gold border and a warm magenta-gold arrow glyph.

nodes.name: OnboardingNavArrow
nodes.summary: Adaptive drop-in wrapper selecting the dark or light nav arrow by colorScheme and mirroring horizontally for forward direction; the single onboarding navigation entry point.

nodes.name: OBSheetChrome.swift
nodes.summary: Shared ViewModifier giving the onboarding custom sheets native-style chrome: full-bleed dark surface with continuous rounded top corners and a spectrum border that traces the top edge and dissolves down the sides.
nodes.languageNotes: Exposed via an `obSheetChrome(widen:)` View extension; uses layered LinearGradient masks to taper the spectrum border off both axes.

nodes.name: OnboardingFooter.swift
nodes.summary: Small footer label shown beneath the CTA on onboarding screens, carrying privacy/encryption reassurance copy at minimum-legible Dynamic Type size.

nodes.name: OnboardingNavBar.swift
nodes.summary: Reusable onboarding top row pairing an optional circular back button with a centered OnboardingProgressBar; used at the head of every onboarding screen showing navigation.

nodes.name: RacetrackTabBar.swift
nodes.summary: Main app tab bar binding to AppTab: a capsule of icon pills with a sequenced racetrack-trim border animation (old pill erases while new pill draws) over a shimmering glass background.
nodes.languageNotes: Bar owns all per-tab trim values so it can coordinate the reverse-then-forward handoff without pills fighting; animation guarded by an isAnimating lock.

nodes.name: RacetrackTabBar
nodes.summary: Tab bar view that binds the selected AppTab and runs the coordinated draw/erase border sequence on tab change.

nodes.name: SafariView.swift
nodes.summary: Thin UIViewControllerRepresentable wrapper over SFSafariViewController for presenting hosted legal web pages in-app via a sheet.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges UIKit's SFSafariViewController into SwiftUI; iOS 26 compliant (WKWebView-backed Safari controller, not the banned UIWebView).

nodes.name: TabContentWrapper.swift
nodes.summary: Generic wrapper applied to every tab's content that adds bottom scroll inset, a gradient fade mask, and indicator inset so content dissolves cleanly behind the floating tab bar.

nodes.name: OnboardingProgressBar.swift
nodes.summary: Heavily-tuned onboarding progress bar with a gradient fill, traveling shimmer, and a multi-layer Canvas bloom + particle completion effect; ships extracted pure-math helpers, a testable animation clock, and full accessibility/RTL/localization support.
nodes.languageNotes: Animation math and clock are factored out of the View into a pure enum and an @Observable class for unit-testability; a 30fps TimelineView drives the bloom and is paused on background.

nodes.name: AnimationMath
nodes.summary: UI-free namespace of pure functions computing shimmer phase, bloom/breathe intensity, shimmer X offset, and a NaN-safe progress ratio.

nodes.name: ProgressAnimationClock
nodes.summary: @Observable clock tracking an activation timestamp and computing elapsed seconds, extracted so the bar's animation state is testable and identity-safe.

nodes.name: OnboardingProgressBar
nodes.summary: The progress bar View itself: derives fill geometry, branches dark/light/RTL gradients, and composes the static bar plus the TimelineView-driven bloom canvas and particle system on completion.

nodes.name: OrbitIndicator.swift
nodes.summary: Reusable three-state (pending → processing → complete) loading indicator drawing an animated comet-tail orbit via a TimelineView Canvas, with a gradient fill, glow, and holographic sheen for the complete state.
nodes.languageNotes: Spark head uses .color shading rather than radialGradient because the latter is silently dropped by Xcode's preview canvas renderer.

nodes.name: OrbitIndicator
nodes.summary: Public three-state orbit indicator View that layers a pending ring, animated orbit canvas, complete gradient fill, glow, and a sweeping holographic sheen.

nodes.name: OrbitIndicatorState
nodes.summary: Equatable enum of the indicator's lifecycle: pending (static ring), processing (animated orbit), complete (filled + glow).

nodes.name: ProgressBar.swift
nodes.summary: Minimal value/max horizontal capsule progress bar filled with the spectrum border gradient.

nodes.name: ProgressRingView.swift
nodes.summary: Reusable circular progress ring with a spectrum-stroked arc and a centered percentage label, sized and weighted by init parameters.

nodes.name: ScoreRing.swift
nodes.summary: Animated score ring rendering a 0-100 score as an angular spectrum sweep with a centered numeric label that fills in on appear.

nodes.name: ScreenshotProtectionModifier.swift
nodes.summary: ViewModifier that obscures private content with a material overlay when the screen is being captured or screenshotted, exposed via a screenshotProtected() View extension.
nodes.languageNotes: Observes UIApplication.userDidTakeScreenshot and UIScreen.capturedDidChange notifications; resolves the active window scene rather than using the banned UIScreen.main.

nodes.name: SpectrumBar.swift
nodes.summary: Trivial horizontal spectrum-gradient capsule used as a thin divider/accent bar.

nodes.name: GradientText.swift
nodes.summary: Static two-tone gradient text view (no animation), colorScheme-aware; the non-animated counterpart to LivingText's premium three-tone treatment.

nodes.name: KeywordHighlightText.swift
nodes.summary: Text view that builds an AttributedString highlighting specified keywords in accent colors (cyan/magenta/gold) against a base color for prompt emphasis.

nodes.name: LivingText.swift
nodes.summary: Premium animated tri-color 'breathing' headline text: a TimelineView layers screen-blended bloom glows and a subtle scale breath over a full-opacity spectrum core, with a static Reduce Motion fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Per-frame math is moved into an explicitly-typed Frame struct resolved in init so the TimelineView closure stays cheap to type-check (was ~216ms inline).

nodes.name: LivingText
nodes.summary: Animated breathing-gradient headline View with three screen-blended Text layers (outer bloom, inner glow, crisp core) driven by a TimelineView.

nodes.name: SpectrumBulletRow.swift
nodes.summary: Labeled list row marked by a flat spectrum disc with a periodic holographic specular sweep; intentionally not a checkmark so lists read as possibilities rather than guaranteed deliverables.
nodes.languageNotes: phaseOffset (by index) staggers the sweep so a list cascades rather than flashing in lockstep; sweep gated off under Reduce Motion.

nodes.name: SignInView.swift
nodes.summary: Sign-in screen presenting the Vayl wordmark, a Sign in with Apple CTA wired to AuthService, loading/error states, and tappable Terms/Privacy links that open in an in-app SafariView sheet.
nodes.languageNotes: Intercepts custom vayl-legal:// markdown links via an OpenURLAction so legal links open the SafariView sheet instead of leaving the app.

nodes.name: SignInView
nodes.summary: Auth entry View that forwards Apple sign-in to its injected AuthService and reflects the service's loading/error state.

nodes.name: CompanionCardStore.swift
nodes.summary: Stub @Observable @MainActor Store that maps a couple's DesireMatches to companion cards pointing at a conversation or deck to open together; returns placeholder content until real companion data and deck mapping are wired.

nodes.name: CompanionCardStore
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor store producing placeholder CompanionCards from a list of DesireMatches.

nodes.name: DesireMapStore.swift
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor Store for the Desire Map rater: resolves the cohort track from the local SwiftData profile, loads track items, upserts one DesireMapEntry per (userId, itemId), marks the profile complete, and best-effort syncs on completion.
nodes.languageNotes: Local-first: completion flag is written to SwiftData independently of remote sync; uses #Predicate FetchDescriptors and a UserDefaults pending-sync retry flag.

nodes.name: DesireMapStore
nodes.summary: Rater store owning items, ratings, track, and load/rate/sync logic backed by SwiftData and ContentLoader.

nodes.name: DesireRevealStore.swift
nodes.summary: @Observable @MainActor Store for the D4 reveal: fetches the couple's alignment-only matches via DesireSyncService and resolves the free/locked split against the EntitlementStore (the conversion gate).
nodes.languageNotes: Read path is privacy-scoped (alignment + is_free_reveal only, never raw partner answers); defines a Phase enum and a RevealMatch view-model struct; unlockAll() runs the Core purchase.

nodes.name: DesireRevealStore
nodes.summary: Reveal store that loads matches and computes unlocked/locked partitions based on Core entitlement.

nodes.name: RevealMatch
nodes.summary: Identifiable view-model for one reveal row carrying display name, alignment, locked flag, and a celebratory subtitle — no raw answers or gap.

nodes.name: DesireMapView.swift
nodes.summary: View layer for the two-track Desire Map card rater: reads DesireMapStore, presents one desire per card with four cohort-driven answers, routes between rater/completion/empty states, and is screenshot-protected.

nodes.name: DesireMapView
nodes.summary: Rater View that forwards each rating choice to the store and advances through the deck of desire items.

nodes.name: DesireRevealView.swift
nodes.summary: View layer for the D4 reveal 'magic moment': reads DesireRevealStore, celebrates aligned matches with staggered reveal animations, shows locked teasers with a price-hint unlock CTA, and is screenshot-protected.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads the EntitlementStore from the environment for the price hint; staggered enter animations respect Reduce Motion.

nodes.name: DesireRevealView
nodes.summary: Reveal View that routes the store's loading/ready/empty/failed phases and forwards unlock and request actions.

nodes.name: CardChestContainer.swift
nodes.summary: Home-tab container hosting the card deck carousel that morphs between a floating hero state and a framed glass 'chest' (ambient orbs, rim light, matched-geometry header, progress bar) as the carousel phase changes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses matchedGeometryEffect to transition the deck name/fraction between floating and chest states; an 8s breath cycle matches the home dashboard's ambient rate.

nodes.name: CardChestContainer
nodes.summary: Container View wrapping CardCarousel and rendering the morphing chest surface keyed off the carousel's CarouselPhase.

nodes.name: NoiseTexture
nodes.summary: Canvas view scattering randomized single-pixel white dots at low opacity to add film-grain texture over a surface.
